It was informed that in the 31st CMETS NR meeting held on 27.06.2024, it was proposed to grant connectivity to NTPC Renewable at Barmer-II PS at 220 kV level
through S/c line. M/s NTPC informed that they have filed additional applications for about 900 MW in last two months at Fatehgarh-IV PS & requested CTUIL to
grant connectivity at 400 kV combinedly for all applications. Regarding this, CTUIL in the 31st CMETS NR meeting had clarified that, as the present application is
for 200 MW, connectivity cannot be granted at 400 kV at this stage. However, when the future applications come up for discussion in the subsequent CMETS
meetings for grant & if those applications can be accommodated to Barmer-II PS as per application priority, then the connectivity can be granted at 400 kV based
on t

ified that, as the present application is
for 200 MW, connectivity cannot be granted at 400 kV at this stage. However, when the future applications come up for discussion in the subsequent CMETS
meetings for grant & if those applications can be accommodated to Barmer-II PS as per application priority, then the connectivity can be granted at 400 kV based
on total connectivity quantum at Barmer-II PS. Therefore, at that stage connectivity was considered for grant at 220 kV only. NTPC requested to take up all
applications together in next CMETS meeting for optimization of DTL. After deliberations, it was decided that the above application shall be again taken up for
discussion in the subsequent CMETS NR meeting along with the other applications of NTPC in Fatehgarh-Barmer complex.
M/s NTPC in the present meeting informed that they have decided to withdraw the present application due to huge mismatch between start date of connectivity at
Barmer-II PS & PPA/LOA timeline. The same was confirmed by NTPC Renewable vide mail dated 06.08.2024. Accordingly, the application is decided to be closed
as per Connectivity & GNA Regulations 2022.
